prompt: Cinematic wide-angle landscape photograph at golden hour, depicting a large green hillside covered in multiple winding dirt footpaths. Every individual footpath, when followed closely, clearly rises upward from left to right, with visible incline and ascending stone steps. Yet the overall hillside and mountain mass unmistakably descend from left to right toward a lower valley in the far distance. Both the local upward slopes and the global downward mountain shape are simultaneously visible and unambiguous in the same frame. The viewer’s eye is pulled upward by the paths and downward by the landscape at the same time, creating a quiet but undeniable sense of contradiction. Mood: spatial wrongness, calm inevitability, subtle unease. Color palette: warm amber sunlight, deep green grass, hazy blue distant valley. Photorealistic, natural terrain textures. No text, no charts, no arrows, no labels, no annotations, no infographic elements.
visual_logic: Each local path visibly climbs upward while the overall mountain descends, making local and global trends simultaneously contradictory in one landscape.
